note: Manual prompt-quality gate for the Answerer stage. Checks STRUCTURE and BOUNDS (required fields, value enums, sensible non-empty agent_steps for high-info cases, escalation for low-info cases) — not exact content (model variance makes string matches brittle). Uses real Sonnet 4.6, ~one query each, so this script costs more than the Interpreter/Evaluator gates (~$0.10/run). Not in CI — run manually before any NEW_PIPELINE flag change.

fixtures.description: Strong search context for a known Zapier issue → expect non-empty steps, confidence high/medium, no escalation

input.cleanedQuestion: Zapier integration stopped syncing leads after a tenant migration

confluence.text:
[Confluence] Zapier integration — common issues after tenant migration
The API access toggle in Tenant Settings → Integrations can be reset to OFF during migration. Re-enabling restores sync within ~10 minutes.
https://example.atlassian.net/wiki/x

slack.text:
[#integrations] Customer Zapier broke after migration — turned out to be API access toggle. Re-enabled, sync resumed in ~10 minutes.
https://slack.com/archives/x/y

fixtures.description: Sparse search context for a vague symptom → expect either a clarifying_question short-circuit (preferred) OR a full response with low confidence / escalation

[Confluence] Procore webhook reliability runbook
Intermittent 502s are usually traced to Procore's upstream proxy. Check the X-Procore-Request-ID header against their support portal.
